content: Type 2 diabetes affects over 537 million adults globally, projected to reach 783 million by 2045. Prediabetes (impaired fasting glucose or impaired glucose tolerance) affects 1 in 3 US adults, with 15-30% progressing to diabetes within 5 years without intervention. Diabetes prevention targets modifiable risk factors: obesity (especially central adiposity), physical inactivity, unhealthy diet, and social determinants. The Diabetes Prevention Program (DPP) landmark trial demonstrated 58% risk reduction through lifestyle intervention (7% weight loss, 150 min/week activity) versus 31% with metformin. Prevention strategies include primary prevention (preventing onset in at-risk individuals), secondary prevention (early detection through screening), and tertiary prevention (complication prevention in diagnosed patients). Social-ecological models recognize individual behavior occurs within environmental contexts requiring multi-level interventions.
